input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="12">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                               TextColor="Gray" />
                    </VerticalStackLayout>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>

    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<ItemModel> _items = new();

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api) => _api = api;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChanged()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}",
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;            // clear so re-selection works
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Property | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | Drives the list; use `ObservableCollection` so adds/removes auto-update the UI |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Only one item selectable at a time |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` | Two-way by default — lets the VM read the pick *and* clear it |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires on tap; navigates then nulls `SelectedItem` so the same row can be tapped again |
| `RefreshView.Command` → `RefreshCommand` | Triggered on pull-down gesture |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | Set `false` when done to dismiss the spinner |

**Tip:** Register the page and route in `AppShell.xaml.cs`:
```csharp
Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(DetailPage), typeof(DetailPage));
```

This uses the **MVVM Community Toolkit** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate. If you're not using it, you'd implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` and `ICommand` manually.
